title: Johnson & Johnson — Hematology — Synthesis
html:
J&J Hematology Cross-Study Synthesis

Generated: 2026-04-24 Studies analyzed: TECVAYLI KPI Launch Tracker (weekly R4W through Apr 22), MM Bispecifics ATU Tableau, Heme MSL Effectiveness Kickoff, TEC-DARA 2L ITP Followups, Heme MM MEE Discussion Points Data sources: SharePoint deliverables + J&J earnings transcripts (Q3-2025 through Q1-2026)

1. Cross-Study Patterns

Pattern 1: TEC-DARA Is Winning the 2L Awareness Battle — But CARVYKTI Wins the Preference Battle

KPI Tracker (Apr 22): TEC-DARA unaided and aided awareness in 2L+ MM "remains stable and significantly outperforms competitors." Prescribing intent is steady.

Same KPI Tracker — Patient Appropriateness: CARVYKTI leads TEC-DARA in 2L physician appropriateness: 57% vs 52%. In patients <75 years old, CARVYKTI leads even more clearly (63% vs 56%). TEC-DARA only leads in CD38-naive patients (35% vs 24%) and high-risk disease (63% vs 62%).

Bispecifics ATU: TEC-D awareness rising (44% → 53% unaided over 6 weeks), but ITP constrained by "niche patient type views" — physicians see TEC-DARA as appropriate for specific profiles, not for all 2L patients.

What this means: J&J has the awareness. They don't have the default preference in 2L. The gap is physician belief about which patients get bispecific vs CAR-T — and right now, oncologists default to CAR-T for younger, fitter patients and reserve TEC-DARA for older, high-risk, CD38-sensitive patients. The TAM for TEC-DARA in 2L is being defined narrowly by physicians.

Pattern 2: The Shift Source Is Clear — Kyprolis and DARA-Triplets Are Being Cannibalized

KPI Tracker (Slide 3): Physicians intending TEC-DARA in 2L report shifting from:

  • Darzalex-based triplets (DRd, DKd, DVd, DPd): 40%
  • Kyprolis-based regimens without anti-CD38 (KRd): 31%
  • Other triplets: 13%
  • CAR-T: only 6%

What this means: TEC-DARA is NOT taking share from CARVYKTI — it's replacing older regimens. This is good news for J&J's portfolio (no internal cannibalization) but confirms that the bispecific-vs-CAR-T question remains unresolved. Physicians are using TEC-DARA for patients who WOULD HAVE gotten a traditional regimen, not for patients who would have gotten CAR-T.

Pattern 3: Community Expansion Is Real — But the Measurement Hasn't Caught Up

Earnings Q1 2026: Both TECVAYLI (+30.1%) and TALVEY (+72.8%) growth attributed to "community setting expansion." CARVYKTI growth (+57.4%) driven by "site expansion."

MSL Kickoff: Study designed for 240 interactions split between NDMM (40 DARZALEX), RRMM (60 bispecific), and CAR-T (40). Includes academic vs community split. But the study was kicked off June 2025 — results targeted Oct 2025. No final report visible on SharePoint yet.

KPI Tracker: Sample size of ~60 oncologists per R4W. No visible community vs academic segmentation in the available slides.

What this means: J&J is claiming community expansion on earnings calls, but ZoomRx's current tracking isn't designed to quantify it. The MSL study should have community data, but the final report isn't in the SharePoint share folder. This is a measurement gap AND a BD opportunity — propose a community-segmented bispecific adoption tracker.

Pattern 4: The Franchise Architecture Is Unprecedented — No Other Company Owns All 4 Modalities

Portfolio reality: J&J owns anti-CD38 (DARZALEX), BCMA bispecific (TECVAYLI), GPRC5D bispecific (TALVEY), and BCMA CAR-T (CARVYKTI). No competitor has this breadth:

  • Pfizer has Elrexfio (bispecific) only
  • BMS has Abecma (CAR-T) only
  • Sanofi has Sarclisa (anti-CD38) only

Earnings Q1 2026: DARZALEX $4.0B + CARVYKTI $600M + TECVAYLI (est ~$230M based on 30% growth from $177M) + TALVEY (est ~$210M based on 73% growth from $122M) = ~$5.0B quarterly MM franchise. This is ~$20B annualized in multiple myeloma alone.

What this means: J&J doesn't just want to win individual drug battles — they want to own the entire treatment sequence. The strategic question for ZoomRx is: how do you help a client optimize cannibalization within their own portfolio? This is a study type that doesn't exist yet.

2. Thesis Check

Thesis Status Evidence
1. TEC-DARA reshapes 2L STRENGTHENED FDA approved, 52% physician appropriateness, physicians shifting from DARA-triplets/Kyprolis
2. DARZALEX is the annuity CONFIRMED $14.4B FY2025, +17.8% Q1, 12-point frontline gains
3. Bispecific vs CAR-T sequencing is THE question STRONGLY CONFIRMED 57% vs 52% preference split; shift sources show no CARVYKTI cannibalization; no resolution in sight
4. Community adoption is the volume unlock STRENGTHENED BUT UNQUANTIFIED Earnings cite community expansion; ZoomRx data doesn't segment it yet
5. TALVEY is underappreciated portfolio insurance STRENGTHENED +72.8% growth, 76% appropriate for 4L+, unique GPRC5D mechanism

3. Contradictions & Surprises

  1. CARVYKTI at $600M/quarter is a $2.4B run rate — this product barely gets mentioned on the ZoomRx radar (MSL kickoff allocates only 40 CAR-T interactions out of 240). Yet it's growing faster than TECVAYLI in absolute dollars.

  2. TEC-DARA is NOT taking share from CARVYKTI. Only 6% of physicians shifting to TEC-DARA are coming from CAR-T. If J&J expected TEC-DARA to reduce CARVYKTI cannibalization concerns, that's validated — but it also means the bispecific and CAR-T markets are growing independently, not competing.

  3. TALVEY rated appropriate for 76% of 4L+ patients — higher than TECVAYLI monotherapy (70%) in the same line. The "portfolio insurance" thesis may understate TALVEY's importance: it's not just last-resort rescue, it's the preferred later-line option.

4. Gaps — What's Missing

  1. No community vs academic segmentation in the KPI tracker. Earnings claim community expansion; we can't quantify it.

  2. MSL effectiveness study results are missing from SharePoint. Kickoff was June 2025, targeted readout Oct 2025. Where are the results? Was the study completed? This is both a data gap and a project management question.

  3. No CARVYKTI-specific tracking. At $600M/quarter, CARVYKTI deserves its own measurement framework — not 40 interactions in a 240-interaction MSL study.

  4. No sequencing data. Which patients get bispecific first vs CAR-T first? What's the real-world treatment pathway? ZoomRx could propose a retrospective sequencing analysis using claims data or a prospective physician decision-tree study.

  5. No infection rate tracking. Hypogammaglobulinemia and infection risk from bispecifics is the #1 safety concern. No current study captures real-world infection burden by treatment.

5. Priority Actions for Vinoth

  1. Find the MSL study results. The Heme MSL Effectiveness study was kicked off June 2025 with an Oct 2025 readout target. The results should exist somewhere. Ask the team (Winston, Aruna) whether results were delivered to J&J and where the final report lives.

  2. Propose a bispecific-vs-CAR-T sequencing study to J&J. This is the highest-value BD opportunity in hematology. No competitor can do this study because only J&J owns both modalities. Frame it as: "We can tell you which physicians choose TECVAYLI first vs CARVYKTI first, why, and what predicts the outcome."

  3. Add community vs academic segmentation to the next KPI tracker refresh. The data infrastructure already exists (Activity Screener captures practice setting). Flag this with the project team as a low-effort, high-value enhancement.

  4. Brief J&J on the "shift source" finding. The fact that TEC-DARA is replacing DARA-triplets and Kyprolis (not CARVYKTI) is a strategically important insight. It means TEC-DARA is expanding the bispecific market, not cannibalizing CAR-T. J&J's commercial team should know this — it affects how they position TEC-DARA messaging.

  5. Propose standalone CARVYKTI tracking. At $600M/quarter and 57% growth, CARVYKTI deserves its own PET or launch tracker. The current allocation (40 interactions in a broader study) is insufficient for a product this large and fast-moving.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
